summary: The codebase is in solid shape with 93 percent backend test coverage and working CI. Several previous findings are resolved: is_domestic consolidation (5 of 6 api files done), GZip middleware added, DB composite index added, confirm() replaced with AlertDialog. Critical remaining items: (1) stocks.py still duplicates _is_domestic() as a skipped step of the consolidation; (2) OrderDialog.tsx at 605 lines covers real trade execution with zero tests; (3) analytics/journal/compare pages (654/537/372 lines) have no test files; (4) the cryptography package powering AES-256 credential encryption is one patch behind. Secondary issues: hardcoded Decimal(1450) in portfolios.py diverges from the centralized constant, three analytics endpoints missing response_model break openapi-typescript generation, alert business logic misplaced in the api layer, and import bisect inlined inside a nested function.

findings.title: _is_domestic() duplicate in stocks.py after consolidation
findings.detail: stocks.py defines its own _DOMESTIC_TICKER_RE and _is_domestic() at lines 27-31 instead of importing from app.core.ticker. Five other api modules were migrated but stocks.py was skipped. Any future rule change diverges silently in the stock search code path.
findings.recommendation: Remove _DOMESTIC_TICKER_RE and _is_domestic() from stocks.py. Add from app.core.ticker import is_domestic. Replace the local call at line 97.

findings.title: place_domestic_order (128 lines) and place_overseas_order (122 lines) exceed 50-line limit
findings.detail: Both placement functions inline validation, header construction, KIS HTTP call, response parsing, error classification, and DB write. Each step is untestable in isolation. check_filled_orders is 94 lines for similar reasons.
findings.recommendation: Extract _build_order_payload(), _call_kis_order_api(), and _persist_order_record() as standalone helpers. Each placement function coordinates helpers and stays under 50 lines.

findings.title: analytics/page.tsx, journal/page.tsx, compare/page.tsx have no test files
findings.detail: Three large pages with complex business logic (FX gain/loss tables, investment journal debounce filtering, portfolio comparison charts) have zero test files. dashboard.test.tsx and portfolio-detail.test.tsx set the precedent but these pages were never covered.
findings.recommendation: Add smoke tests using existing MSW infrastructure: analytics renders FX section only with foreign holdings; journal debounce search filters entries; compare shows empty state with fewer than 2 portfolios.

findings.title: OrderDialog.tsx at 605 lines with no tests covers trading execution
findings.detail: OrderDialog is the primary UI for executing real trades at 605 lines with no test file. Handles market/limit toggle, domestic vs overseas routing, orderable quantity fetch, live price display, validation, and submission. A regression has direct financial consequence.
findings.recommendation: Create OrderDialog.test.tsx with MSW handlers for /orders and /orders/orderable. Cover: limit price field visibility toggle; submit blocked when quantity exceeds orderable; USD display for overseas orders.

findings.title: cryptography package outdated (46.0.5 to 46.0.6) on AES-256 KIS credential path
findings.detail: The cryptography library is one patch behind latest. It performs AES-256 encryption of every KIS API key and secret stored in the database. Patch releases routinely include CVE backports. Running a stale version on the credential encryption layer is elevated risk for a trading application.
findings.recommendation: Run: pip install --upgrade cryptography gunicorn boto3 botocore. Verify pytest passes. Update requirements.txt with pinned versions.

findings.title: Hardcoded Decimal(1450) fallback in portfolios.py diverges from centralized constant
findings.detail: portfolios.py hardcodes Decimal(1450) at lines 271 and 294. The shared constant _FX_FALLBACK_RATE exists in backend/app/services/kis_price.py. If the canonical fallback is updated, portfolios.py P&L for overseas holdings silently drifts.
findings.recommendation: Import _FX_FALLBACK_RATE from app.services.kis_price and replace both Decimal(1450) literals in portfolios.py with the shared constant.

findings.title: Three analytics endpoints missing response_model, breaking openapi-typescript generation
findings.detail: GET /metrics, GET /fx-gain-loss, and GET /krw-asset-history have no response_model on their @router.get decorators. FastAPI omits these schemas from OpenAPI output so npm run generate:types produces unknown types for all three in src/types/api.ts, negating the openapi-typescript investment.
findings.recommendation: Create MetricsResponse, FxGainLossItem, KrwAssetHistoryPoint Pydantic models in backend/app/schemas/analytics.py and apply as response_model on each decorator.

findings.title: Alert business logic lives in api/alerts.py instead of services layer
findings.detail: check_triggered_alerts() and check_and_dedup_alerts() are pure business logic with no FastAPI HTTP handling but live in app/api/alerts.py. dashboard.py and prices.py import them, creating lateral api-layer cross-dependencies that violate layering and make functions harder to test standalone.
findings.recommendation: Move both functions to backend/app/services/alert_service.py. Update imports in dashboard.py, prices.py, and alerts.py.

findings.title: useOrders.ts (260 lines) and useNotifications.ts (99 lines) have no tests
findings.detail: useOrders.ts orchestrates order placement, cancellation, and settlement polling in 260 lines of financial-critical logic. useNotifications.ts drives the bell badge count. Neither has tests while usePriceStream.test.ts demonstrates the renderHook pattern.
findings.recommendation: Add useOrders.test.ts with renderHook() and MSW. Cover: placeOrder appends to pending orders; cancelOrder removes the entry; API error surfaces in hook state.

findings.title: Inline import bisect inside nested function body in analytics.py
findings.detail: import bisect appears at line 527 inside _nearest_fx_rate(), a nested helper called on every get_fx_gain_loss() request. PEP 8 requires all imports at module top level. This hides the bisect dependency from readers scanning module imports.
findings.recommendation: Move import bisect to the top of analytics.py with the other stdlib imports (after datetime, json, math).

findings.title: get_krw_asset_history at 126 lines with forward-fill loop not extracted for reuse
findings.detail: The forward-fill loop at lines 676-681 converts a sparse fx_date_map into a dense date-indexed rate dict. This algorithm will be needed for the benchmark overlay in Milestone 21 but is buried inside a 126-line endpoint handler.
findings.recommendation: Extract forward_fill_rates(dates: list, rate_map: dict, fallback: float) into backend/app/core/fx_utils.py with a unit test, then call from analytics.py.

findings.title: lucide-react major version gap (0.577 to 1.7.0)
findings.detail: lucide-react is at 0.577.0 while 1.7.0 is available, a major version containing breaking icon renames and changed export patterns. Already tracked in todo.md but gap grows each sprint. Prerequisite for TypeScript 6 adoption.
findings.recommendation: Inventory used icons with grep -rn lucide-react frontend/src, cross-reference with the lucide 1.x CHANGELOG, update on a dedicated branch with CI passing before merge.

findings.title: PortfolioHistoryChart uses any[] for Recharts tooltip payload
findings.detail: payload is typed as any[] in CustomTooltipProps and cast to any[] in JSX, with an eslint-disable comment. The actual access pattern payload[0].value shows the shape is fully known at the call site.
findings.recommendation: Define interface RechartsPayloadEntry with value: number and optional name/color fields. Replace both any[] usages and remove the eslint-disable comment.
